channel.title: Comments for page "Archived Pages"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Archived Pages"

item.title: "Legacy Documentation" Category
item.content:encoded:
ngl having 049-ARC being amongst the other -ARC'd SCPs despite being ARC'd for different reasons and being an -ARC of an SCP still on the site is sort of off-putting.

I propose adding a third category to the -ARC skips called "Legacy Documentation" which would fit 049-ARC, and quite possibly any future SCP that would requiring -ARCing after a drastic rewrite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What about the old 1822 ("Colonization Guide")? http://web.archive.org/web/20180625174309/http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-1822 It's needed for the Straight On Till Morning canon and its tales.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think archived SCPs should stay as they are with no further edits, grammar mistakes and all. That's my take on it. Improving them bit by bit would just end up as making them different from how they were archived, which isn't the point; archived stuff should be, well, archived.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would it be wrong to edit archived SCP'S to fix grammar errors the way you would a regular SCP?

item.title: Wait a second
item.content:encoded: Didn't this page used to be linked in the site navigation (along with Decommissioned SCPs)? Why was it removed?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably because -D SCPs are inherently different from -ARC SCPs. Staff was also restructuring the way -D articles would be displayed on the site (I think?) so that might be the reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Decommissioned SCPs is tagged "archived" and appears to be a supplement to SCP Object Classes. Why is it not listed here?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…that button is connected to your Gmail account.

I would vote no for anything non-Wikidot-related unless we're moving away. (Also wrong thread)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is entirely the wrong thread for this. -yoric

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We could do that, but Bland is right. I don't feel confident doing it at all, and— with no offense intended toward anyone else— I don't feel confident asking anyone to do it either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd advise NOT editing the Meta information unless you REALLY know what you're doing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder if we could shove names into meta tags or something and extract them on pages like this one. d_d b_b

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ahh… I gotcha, Quik. My bad. Didn't even think about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Troy, I reverted from the module to manual entry because the module can't list the names of these SCPs, and this is (I believe) the only place where their names are listed. If not for the loss of their names, I would have left it alone.
